commit_message:
Stabilize fetch chunk tests: breaker busy-wait, avoid acquireResponseStream poll leak (#148026)

Fixes flaky tests and a refcount leak in TransportFetchPhaseCoordinationActionTests by waiting on REQUEST circuit breaker usage instead of retrying acquireResponseStream inside assertBusy + expectThrows. Aligns TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests with async processChunk cleanup.

Problem
TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests: processChunk can still run on TRANSPORT_WORKER after the test releases refs; an immediate breaker.getUsed() == 0 can race tearDown and ESTestCase#allBreakersMemoryReleased.
TransportFetchPhaseCoordinationActionTests: assertBusy(() -> expectThrows(ResourceNotFoundException, () -> acquireResponseStream(...))) leaks refs when the stream is still registered (acquire succeeds, expectThrows fails with AssertionError, assertBusy retries without decRef).
Solution
testDoExecuteReleasesRegistrationWhenDataNodeFailsAfterChunkStreaming: replace the busy loop with assertBusy until REQUEST breaker getUsed() == 0 only (no following expectThrows + acquireResponseStream).
testDoExecuteReleasesRegistrationOnLastChunkDeserializationFailure: same breaker assertBusy, then a single expectThrows(ResourceNotFoundException, () -> activeFetchPhaseTasks.acquireResponseStream(...)) to assert deregistration without a retry loop around acquire.
TransportFetchPhaseResponseChunkActionTests: wrap the final breaker release check in assertBusy.

Closes #147812
Closes #147974
Closes #147870
Closes #147967

Unmutes tests.

commit_message:
Remove brittle BWC serialization test (#148016)

InferenceActionRequestTests.testWriteTo_ForHasBeenReroutedChanges() was
testing serialization with a specific transport version, but not using
mutateInstanceForVersion() to ensure that any expected changes to the
object due to other transport versions were accounted for. The behaviour
the test is covering is already covered by the testBwcSerialization()
method on the base class, so the test is unnecessary and can be removed.

Closes #148000

Resolves #148041

commit_message:
Dispose rejected YAML xContent test instances when resampling for NEL (#148152)

YAML xContent roundtrip tests resample instances when serialization contains NEL (U+0085). randomValueOtherThanMany dropped rejected instances without calling dispose, which leaked ref-counted test objects.

Use an explicit resample loop that disposes each rejected candidate before creating the next one; keep the same NEL check and outer finally behavior.

Closes #148084

commit_message:
[ESQL] Fix ExternalParquetCountPushdownIT on Windows (#148377)

The IT built EXTERNAL queries as

    "EXTERNAL \"file://" + path.toAbsolutePath() + "\" | ..."

which on Windows yields

    EXTERNAL "file://C:\build\..."

The QUOTED_STRING grammar in
x-pack/plugin/esql/src/main/antlr/lexer/Expression.g4 only accepts
\t \n \r \" \\ as escape sequences, so any backslash followed by
other characters (e.g. \b) raises "token recognition error".

Switch the four query builders to StoragePath.fileUri(Path), the
existing helper that normalizes Windows backslashes to / and emits
the canonical file:///C:/... URI. Also unmute the four affected
tests.

Two parser regression tests in IcebergParsingTests pin the lexer
behaviour: one asserts the C:\\... URI still throws, the other
asserts file:///C:/... parses cleanly.

Closes #148116
Closes #148117
Closes #148118
Closes #148119

commit_message:
[ESQL] Fix ExternalParquetCountPushdownIT on Windows (#148377)

The IT built EXTERNAL queries as

    "EXTERNAL \"file://" + path.toAbsolutePath() + "\" | ..."

which on Windows yields

    EXTERNAL "file://C:\build\..."

The QUOTED_STRING grammar in
x-pack/plugin/esql/src/main/antlr/lexer/Expression.g4 only accepts
\t \n \r \" \\ as escape sequences, so any backslash followed by
other characters (e.g. \b) raises "token recognition error".

Switch the four query builders to StoragePath.fileUri(Path), the
existing helper that normalizes Windows backslashes to / and emits
the canonical file:///C:/... URI. Also unmute the four affected
tests.

Two parser regression tests in IcebergParsingTests pin the lexer
behaviour: one asserts the C:\\... URI still throws, the other
asserts file:///C:/... parses cleanly.

Closes #148116
Closes #148117
Closes #148118
Closes #148119

commit_message:
Skip `testTracingCrossCluster` on release and upgrade builds (#148195)

`testTracingCrossCluster` (added/unmuted in #147680) calls `GET
/_flush_telemetry`, a route exposed only by the test-only
`test-apm-integration` plugin. Two CI paths break:

- **`release-tests` / `checkpart4_release-tests`** (`-Dbuild.snapshot=false`): the test plugin isn't loaded into the cluster, so the route 400s with `Invalid index name [_flush_telemetry]`.
- **`bcUpgradeTest` / `bcUpgradeTestPart4`**: this test doesn't actually consume the BWC distribution (it builds its own current-version clusters), so running it under `bcUpgradeTest` adds no coverage and just doubles the failure surface.

**Changes**

- `RemoteClusterSecurityWithApmTracingRestIT.testTracingCrossCluster`: `assumeTrue("requires test-apm-integration which is only loaded in snapshot builds", Build.current().isSnapshot())` — skip cleanly on non-snapshot builds instead of failing.
- `x-pack/plugin/security/qa/multi-cluster/build.gradle`: restrict `bcUpgradeTest` to only the tests that actually consume the BWC distribution (`RemoteClusterSecurityBWCToRCS{1,2}ClusterRestIT`). The other ~38 test classes in this module — including `RemoteClusterSecurityWithApmTracingRestIT` — build their own current-version clusters and were silently rerunning under `bcUpgradeTest` / `bc-upgrade-tests-part4` / `pr-upgrade-part-4` without exercising any BWC behavior. 

Closes #148189.

issue_title: ESQL: SUM(x+constant) and other aggs not respecting multi-values
issue_body:
The optimization from https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/148070 unfortunately introduces breaking behavior, where e.g. `STATS SUM(x + 1)` unrolls multi-values _before_ applying the `+1`.

This is
- inconsistent with previous versions of ES. In case of a multivalue, `SUM(x + 1)` would ignore the multi-value (as `x+1` is not defined when `x` is a mv) and return a warning to the user, and
- inconsistent in itself, because `STATS SUM(x+1)` should be equivalent to `EVAL tmp = x+1 | STATS SUM(tmp)`, which ignores mvs and returns a warning.

commit_message:
Prevent race between watch firing and cleanup in test (#148361)

In `testThatLoadingWithNonExistingIndexWorks` the test
sets up a watch to fire every second, checks that it ran
once, and then exits. The watch is still present during
cleanup, and can race with the existing cleanup code. If
it runs again during cleanup it can recreate the watch-history
index after it has been deleted, failing the test.

Fixes #148323

issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

9.4.0

### Installed Plugins

### Java Version

openjdk version "21.0.11" 2026-04-21

### OS Version

Linux tlog-server 6.12.85+deb13-amd64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Debian 6.12.85-1 (2026-04-30) x86_64 GNU/Linux

### Problem Description

After running `apt update && apt upgrade --yes` on my Debian machine, I upgraded my ELK stack from 9.3.4 to 9.4.0:

```
00:01:31.910  Unpacking elasticsearch (9.4.0) over (9.3.4) ...
00:01:43.104  Unpacking kibana (9.4.0) over (9.3.4) ...

00:03:00.231  Unpacking metricbeat (9.4.0) over (9.3.4) ...
```

From that point kibana, logstash and metricbeat worked - while elasticsearch failed to start. `systemctl status elasticsearch` produced:

```
The current machine does not support all of the following CPU features that are required by the image: [CX8, CMOV, FXSR, MMX, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSSE3, SSE4_1, SSE4_2,>
Please rebuild the executable with an appropriate setting of the -march option.
```

From inspecting `/proc/cpuinfo` It seems my machine does support all of the above flags - BUT `SSE3` is listed as `pni` ([Prescott New Instructions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SSE3) - Intel's name for `SSE3`).

commit_message:
Pin server-launcher native -march to x86-64-v2 (#148542)

This PR pins -march=compatibility on the x86_64 task — GraalVM's AMD64-baseline mode (CX8/CMOV/FXSR/MMX/SSE/SSE2 only) — so the binary runs on any 64-bit x86 CPU, verified under qemu-x86_64 across every model from Core 2 Duo through Skylake-Server.
